---
description: 'Instructions for managing tasks using todo.txt format with GTD methodology'
applyTo: 'my/todo.md'
---
# Todo Instructions

## Task Format
- [ ] (Priority) Task description `@Context` `+Project` due:YYYY-MM-DD

## Priority System (Eisenhower Matrix)
- `(A)`: Urgent & Important - Immediate attention required (deadlines, emergencies)
- `(B)`: Important but Not Urgent - Strategic tasks (planning, skill development)
- `(C)`: Urgent but Not Important - Interruptions (most meetings, many emails)
- `(D)`: Not Urgent & Not Important - Time wasters (to be minimized)

## List Structure

### 🍅 Now/Pomodoro (1)
Current focus task. Maximum one item (two if absolutely necessary).

### 📆 Today
Tasks committed to complete today. Move from "This Week" during morning planning.

### 🗓 This Week
Weekly commitments. Move from "Later" during weekly review.

### 🗒 Later
Actionable tasks committed to do, but not within the next week.

### ⏳ Waiting For (delegated)
Tasks delegated to others or waiting on dependencies.

### 🤔 Someday/Maybe
Non-committal ideas and future possibilities. Review quarterly.

### ✅ Completed
Finished tasks. Archive/delete during weekly review.

## Best Practices
1. Break projects into actionable tasks
2. Use contexts (@home, @phone, @email) for task grouping
3. Review and update lists daily (Today) and weekly (This Week)
4. Move stale Later tasks to Someday/Maybe
5. Keep Now/Pomodoro focused on single current task
